content_of_post_to_answer: How can regular walking help your physical and mental health?
answer: Walking is often underestimated because people measure it only by calories burned. Its deeper benefit is that it changes the state of the mind while the body is moving: repetitive steps create space for thoughts to settle, stress hormones to ease, and attention to recover. I’ve explored this connection between simple habits and mental clarity in more detail on my profile, if it’s useful. A walk is not just exercise; it is a reset button for the systems we constantly overload.
